name: the primitive type called yew

VINIT.justification: The state before any phase angle or symmetry axis is defined.

TANCH.justification: The closed circle of the U(1) group containing all possible phase rotations.

AFWD.justification: A forward shift in the angular coordinate of the symmetry state.

AREV.justification: A reverse shift or complex conjugation of the phase state.

CLINK.justification: The composition of multiple phase shifts into a single resultant angle.

IMSCRIB.justification: The zero-degree rotation where the state recognizes its own orientation.

FSPLIT.justification: A single phase state splitting into a leading edge and a trailing edge.

FFUSE.justification: The recombination of split phase paths to recover the original coherent wave.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative alignment of phases resulting in signal gain.

EVALF.justification: The negative alignment of phases resulting in signal cancellation.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where multiple phase angles are held simultaneously without collapse.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of a specific phase angle in memory.

steps.domain_action: bifurcate the signal into dual paths
verify constructive alignment on the primary path
compose the primary path with a secondary rotation
apply a parity flip to the secondary path
verify destructive alignment on the secondary path
recombine paths into a single interference result
hold the resulting phase in superposition
record the final phase lock to the ledger
seal the operation within the U(1) boundary
